steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage gives two dated examples (Oklahoma 1987, Maine 1999) and then makes a general claim: the blank introduces the statement that years pass between selection and designation. The blank must signal that this time gap is a common occurrence — not a logical conclusion, not a similarity, and not an emphasis.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Evaluate all four choices:
  • Choice A — ‘In many cases,’: signals frequency, fitting the claim that years commonly pass between selection and official designation. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Similarly,’: signals similarity to a preceding point, but the sentence is not parallel to anything stated before it — it introduces a new general pattern. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Indeed,’: signals emphasis or confirmation of something already established, but the time-gap claim is new information, not a restatement. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Therefore,’: signals a conclusion caused by prior reasoning, but the Oklahoma/Maine examples do not logically produce this conclusion — they merely illustrate it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
